name: World Geography
description: Mountains, rivers, capitals, and continents — how well do you know planet Earth?
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital and largest city of France. Even if you've never been, you've definitely seen it in movies!

questions.question: Which continent is Brazil located on?
questions.explanation: Brazil is in South America — it's the largest country on the continent. Loads of South Africans have gone there for the 2014 World Cup!

questions.question: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean covers more than 165 million square kilometres — larger than all the land on Earth combined. Makes our two oceans look like rock pools!

questions.question: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.explanation: It's not Sydney! Canberra is the capital. They built it as a compromise because Sydney and Melbourne couldn't stop fighting over the honour. Classic sibling rivalry.

questions.question: Which country is known as the 'Land of the Rising Sun'?
questions.explanation: Japan's name in Japanese (Nihon/Nippon) literally means 'origin of the sun'. The rising sun also features on their flag.

questions.question: The Amazon River flows through which continent?
questions.explanation: The Amazon flows through South America, mainly through Brazil and Peru. It carries more water than any other river on the planet.

questions.question: Mount Everest is located on the border of which two countries?
questions.explanation: Everest sits on the Nepal-China (Tibet) border. At 8,849 metres, it's the highest point on Earth. Table Mountain is a bit more manageable at 1,085m!

questions.question: Which European country has the shape of a boot?
questions.explanation: Italy is famously shaped like a boot, kicking the island of Sicily. Once you see it, you can never unsee it.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world by area?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is just 0.44 square kilometres — smaller than most golf courses. You could fit about 3,800 of them inside eThekwini!

questions.question: Which river runs through Egypt and is essential to the country's agriculture?
questions.explanation: The Nile has been the lifeline of Egyptian civilisation for thousands of years. Without it, Egypt would basically be all desert.

questions.question: Which is the only continent with land in all four hemispheres?
questions.explanation: Africa spans the Northern, Southern, Eastern, and Western hemispheres. Our continent is properly in the middle of everything!

questions.question: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.explanation: Ottawa is the capital, not Toronto or Vancouver as many people guess. It was chosen partly because it was further from the US border — safety first!

questions.question: The Great Wall of China is approximately how long?
questions.explanation: The Great Wall stretches about 21,196 km including all its branches. That's roughly the distance from Cape Town to Tokyo and back!

questions.question: Arrange these mountains from TALLEST to SHORTEST:
questions.explanation: Everest (8,849m) > K2 (8,611m) > Kangchenjunga (8,586m) > Lhotse (8,516m). All four are in the Himalayas and over 8,500m tall. Table Mountain didn't make the cut, sorry!

questions.question: Which country has the most UNESCO World Heritage Sites?
questions.explanation: Italy holds the record with 59 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, just ahead of China. South Africa has 10 — not too shabby for our side!

questions.question: What is the driest inhabited continent on Earth?
questions.explanation: Australia is the driest inhabited continent, with an average annual rainfall of just 470mm. Antarctica is drier, but nobody lives there permanently.

questions.question: The Danube River flows through more European capitals than any other river. How many capitals does it pass through?
questions.explanation: The Danube flows through 4 capitals: Vienna (Austria), Bratislava (Slovakia), Budapest (Hungary), and Belgrade (Serbia). That's a proper European road trip on water!

questions.question: Which African country has the largest land area?
questions.explanation: Algeria is the largest country in Africa at about 2.38 million sq km. It's also the largest country in the Arab world and the Mediterranean basin.

questions.question: Which strait separates Africa from Europe?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Morocco (Africa) from Spain (Europe) — at its narrowest, it's only 14.3 km wide. You can literally see across on a clear day!

questions.question: What is the largest island in the world (excluding continent-sized Australia)?
questions.explanation: Greenland is the world's largest island at about 2.17 million sq km. Despite its name, it's mostly covered in ice. Classic false advertising!

questions.question: Which country is home to the ancient city of Petra, carved into rose-coloured rock?
questions.explanation: Petra is in Jordan and was built by the Nabataeans over 2,000 years ago. It's one of the New Seven Wonders of the World — and it looks unreal in photos!

questions.question: Which two countries share the longest international border in the world?
questions.explanation: The Canada-USA border stretches about 8,891 km (including the Great Lakes). That's longer than the distance from Cape Town to Cairo!

questions.question: What is the capital of Myanmar (formerly Burma)?
questions.explanation: Naypyidaw became Myanmar's capital in 2006, replacing Yangon. It's a massive planned city that was built from scratch — and it's famously almost empty.

questions.question: The Atacama Desert, one of the driest places on Earth, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: The Atacama is in northern Chile. Some weather stations there have NEVER recorded rainfall. It makes the Karoo look like a tropical rainforest!

questions.question: Which sea is the lowest body of water on Earth's surface?
questions.explanation: The Dead Sea sits at about 430 metres below sea level. It's so salty you float effortlessly — nature's own swimming pool hack.

questions.question: Which country contains the most active volcanoes?
questions.explanation: Indonesia has about 130 active volcanoes, more than any other country. It sits on the Pacific Ring of Fire. Rather them than us, hey!

questions.question: Lake Baikal in Russia is famous for being the world's ___?
questions.explanation: Lake Baikal is the deepest lake on Earth at 1,642 metres and holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen freshwater. It's also the oldest lake, at roughly 25 million years.

questions.question: Which Central American country does NOT have a Pacific coastline?
questions.explanation: Belize only has a Caribbean (Atlantic) coastline. It's the only Central American country that doesn't touch the Pacific Ocean.

questions.question: Arrange these rivers from LONGEST to SHORTEST:
questions.explanation: Amazon (6,400 km) > Yangtze (6,300 km) > Mississippi (6,275 km) > Yellow River (5,464 km). The Nile is longer than all of these, but it wasn't invited to this party.

questions.question: Which European country is divided into cantons?
questions.explanation: Switzerland is made up of 26 cantons, each with a high degree of autonomy. Think of them like SA's provinces, but with more chocolate and ski slopes.

questions.question: What is the only country in the world that spans both the equator and a tropic?
questions.explanation: Brazil is crossed by both the equator in the north and the Tropic of Capricorn in the south. It's a geographically massive country — fifth largest in the world.

questions.question: The Galápagos Islands, famous for inspiring Darwin's theory of evolution, belong to which country?
questions.explanation: The Galápagos Islands are a province of Ecuador, sitting about 1,000 km off its Pacific coast. Darwin visited in 1835 and the rest is history — literally.

questions.question: Which Asian country is the world's largest archipelago by number of islands?
questions.explanation: Indonesia has over 17,000 islands, making it the world's largest archipelago. Only about 6,000 of them are inhabited though. Imagine the island-hopping potential!

questions.question: What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
questions.explanation: Astana became the capital in 1997, replacing Almaty. It was briefly renamed Nur-Sultan (2019-2022) but reverted back. Almaty is still the largest city though.

questions.question: Which African lake is the largest by surface area?
questions.explanation: Lake Victoria has a surface area of about 68,800 sq km, shared by Tanzania, Uganda, and Kenya. It's the world's largest tropical lake and Africa's pride!

questions.question: The Ural Mountains are traditionally considered the boundary between which two continents?
questions.explanation: The Ural Mountains run through Russia and mark the conventional boundary between Europe and Asia. They stretch about 2,500 km from the Arctic to Kazakhstan.

questions.question: Which country's flag features a cedar tree?
questions.explanation: Lebanon's flag features the famous Cedar of Lebanon, which has been a symbol of the country for thousands of years. Biblical vibes all the way!

questions.question: The Gobi Desert spans which two countries?
questions.explanation: The Gobi covers parts of northern China and southern Mongolia. It's a cold desert — temperatures can drop to -40°C in winter. That's braai-cancelling weather!

questions.question: Which country is home to the world's largest rainforest by area after the Amazon?
questions.explanation: The Congo Rainforest in the DRC is the second largest tropical rainforest, covering about 2 million sq km. It's often called the 'lungs of Africa'.

questions.question: What is the most densely populated country in the world (with a population over 1 million)?
questions.explanation: Bangladesh has about 170 million people in just 147,570 sq km, giving it a density of over 1,100 people per sq km. For comparison, SA has about 49 people per sq km.

questions.question: Arrange these countries from LARGEST to SMALLEST by land area:
questions.explanation: USA (9.83M) > Brazil (8.52M) > India (3.29M) > Argentina (2.78M). South Africa's 1.22 million sq km would slot in after Argentina.

questions.question: The Svalbard archipelago, located in the Arctic Ocean, belongs to which country?
questions.explanation: Svalbard belongs to Norway and sits between mainland Norway and the North Pole. Polar bears outnumber people there — about 3,000 bears vs 2,600 humans!

questions.question: Which country has the most natural lakes in the world?
questions.explanation: Canada has an estimated 879,800 lakes, more than the rest of the world combined. Finland comes second — its nickname is literally 'Land of a Thousand Lakes' (and it has way more than that).

questions.question: What is the only country whose flag is not rectangular or square?
questions.explanation: Nepal's flag consists of two stacked triangles — it's the only national flag that isn't a quadrilateral. Switzerland's is square, but Nepal's is truly unique.

questions.question: The Kerguelen Islands, also known as the 'Desolation Islands', are a territory of which country?
questions.explanation: The Kerguelen Islands are a French overseas territory in the southern Indian Ocean. They have no permanent population and are one of the most isolated places on Earth.

questions.question: Which African country has the longest coastline on the continent?
questions.explanation: Somalia has Africa's longest coastline at about 3,333 km along the Indian Ocean and Gulf of Aden. SA's 2,798 km coastline comes in not too far behind.

questions.question: What is the highest navigable lake in the world?
questions.explanation: Lake Titicaca, on the Peru-Bolivia border at 3,812m above sea level, is the highest navigable lake for large vessels. Higher lakes exist but can't support commercial navigation.

questions.question: Arrange these capital cities from NORTH to SOUTH by latitude:
questions.explanation: Reykjavik (64°N) > Singapore (1°N) > Brasilia (15°S) > Canberra (35°S). Geography nerds unite — latitude questions are the real flex!

questions.question: Which country contains the geographic centre of all land surfaces on Earth (the centroid of Earth's land)?
questions.explanation: The geographic centre of all land on Earth is calculated to be near Ankara, Turkey (approximately 40°N, 34°E). This takes into account all continents' positions.

questions.question: The Diomede Islands in the Bering Strait are split between the USA and Russia. What is the approximate distance between Big Diomede (Russia) and Little Diomede (USA)?
questions.explanation: Big Diomede and Little Diomede are only 3.8 km apart, but the International Date Line runs between them. So you can technically 'see tomorrow' from one island — that's a proper mind-bender!
